pattern: Because the target values are bounded well under 2^30, only substrings up to 30 characters long can ever matter, so precomputing the shortest, leftmost substring for every value reachable in that window answers every query in constant time.
hints: A query's answer only ever needs a substring whose decimal value fits under roughly 2^30, so no substring longer than about 30 characters is ever worth examining.

Sweep every substring of length 1 up to that cap, from shortest to longest and left to right, and remember the first time each decimal value is produced -- that's automatically the shortest, leftmost substring for that value.
For each query, XOR its two numbers together to get the target value and look it up directly in your precomputed table, returning [-1, -1] when the value was never produced.

edge_inputs.description: single-character string, query that matches it exactly
target value only achievable through a substring with a leading zero, and a shorter substring exists without one
query target has no matching substring anywhere
the same decimal value is produced by two different substrings of equal length, the leftmost occurrence must win
all-zero string, target of zero should resolve to the very first character
multiple queries against the same string mixing found and not-found targets

stress.description: random binary string with random queries whose targets are bounded like the constraints
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    q = max(1, min(2000, size))
    queries = [[rng.randint(0, 1000000000), rng.randint(0, 1000000000)] for _ in range(q)]
    return [s, queries]
